SymbolicExecutionTermLabel, Synchronized, SynchronizedBlock, TermLabelSV, TermSV, Then, ThisConstructorReference, ThisReference, Throw, Throws, Times, TimesAssignment, TransactionStatement, Transformer, Transient, Try, TwoState, TypeCast, TypeDeclaration, TypeOf, TypeOperator, TypeRef, TypeReferenceImp, Unpack, UnsignedShiftRight, UnsignedShiftRightAssignment, UnwindLoop, UpdateApplication, UpdateJunctor, UpdateSV, VariableDeclaration, VariableNamer.IndProgramElementName, VariableReference, VariableSpecification, VariableSV, VisibilityModifier, Volatile, WarySubstOp, While
public interface SyntaxElement

This interface defines the base class for abstract syntax tree structures regardless of the underlying language. All tree-like structures should implement this interface.

For navigating the syntax elements, see SyntaxElementCursor.

If a class has no children, it should implement TerminalSyntaxElement.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    SyntaxElement
    getChild(int n)
    Get the n-th child of this syntax element.
    int
    getChildCount()
     
    default SyntaxElementCursor
    getCursor()
     

  • Method Details

    • getChild

      SyntaxElement getChild(int n)
      Get the n-th child of this syntax element.
      Parameters:
      n - index of the child.
      Returns:
      the n-th child of this syntax element.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if there is no n-th child.

    • getChildCount

      int getChildCount()
      Returns:
      the number of children of this syntax element.

    • getCursor

      default SyntaxElementCursor getCursor()
      Returns:
      a new cursor for the subtree with the current node as root.
